EX 12 – Full Case Picking Changes McLane sells and ships full case quantities of items where each physical case quantity ships as an oLPN . Very often, these items pick from pallet or case flow pick locations which are Case Pick Locations in WMOS. Each unit picked is physically a case of inventory and systemically an oLPN . Today, McLane performs picking in these areas by labels with no real time system interaction. In future, McLane packs oLPN’s through RF Pack Cubed Directed. However, the base available confirmations either require too little detail (Location Only base option) or too many confirmations (SKU and Quantity for example). Therefore, WMOS RF Pack Cubed Directed is enhanced with a new item barcode prompt to pack the entire task detail and a new quantity confirmation option for McLane which allow the user to quickly confirm total or partial count packed. Additionally, the base process prints the oLPN S hipping L abel after the oLPN is packed complete by the user. McLane requires the flexibility to optionally print the oLPN Shipping Labels for all oLPN’s on a task at the start of the task. Therefore, WMOS RF Pack Cubed Directed is enhanced with a new configurable parameter to allow printing at the start of the task. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Pack Cubed Directed – New Quantity Confirmation by SKU and Aggregate Pick Need Reporting N Other N WMOS enhances RF Pack Cubed Directed to pack the aggregate demand of item/pick location combination of the current task at the item barcode prompt or proceed to total quantity prompt . If the user scans item barcode, the p rocess ends and the oLPNs are packed. If the user elects to move to the custom total quantity prompt, the process is to confirm the item barcode and view the total quantity prompt. Th ese custom screen s present the user with two process es as part of this extension: Item Entry or Item and Total Quantity Entry . The initial confirmation of item leverages base item confirmation screen and base validations to confirm the user is picking the correct product , as well as performs custom updates to pack aggregate demand of task for current item/pick location combination . If the item barcode (or alternate options) or the custom Location scan is executed here, WM OS performs all packing updates for the oLPNs associated with this item and pick location combination for this task. WMOS also prints the shipping labels for the packed oLPNs per base. A new custom CTRL key is introduced at the item barcode scan, to move the user to the custom quantity confirmation screen. Once this CTRL key is invoked, the user will confirm the item, then n aggregate quantity across all picking demand from the given pick location and item on the user’s current task. WMOS performs all packing updates and shipping label printing for the quantity entered at this screen. This process allows the user to partially pack the aggregate demand from this item/pick location . As part of this picking confirmation, WMOS prints shipping labels for the packed oLPN’s per base. In order to support exceptions where item scan is not available, a new custom CTRL key is introduced at the item confirmation screen which allows the user to scan location instead. When taken, the CTRL key brings the user to a Location Confirmation screen which leverages base screen and validations to ensure the user is at the correct location for picking. Additionally, as part of this customization, WMOS suppress es oLPN confirmation in the RF Pack Cubed process. Lastly, in addition to the new quantity verification process, McLane requires the ability to optionally print oLPN Shipping Labels at the start of the task rather than as the oLPN packs complete. When configured for custom EX12 start of task printing, WMOS triggers printing for all oLPN’s on the task at the onset of the task. Additionally, WMOS forces the sequencing of both the printing and packing logic to ensure they are aligned. In addition, WMOS suppresses base oLPN ship label printing at the time of oLPN packing complete. Assumptions The base configuration for shortage reason code is configured to default without prompting the user. No customization to the oLPN shipping label which is printed in this process is in scope for this extension. In some instances, McLane leverages the pallet prompt at the start of the packing process, but not always. This is configurable by task. Packing executes based on tasks created with the wave. Stand alone RF Pack Cubed Directed is not used by McLane outside tasking mode. The oLPN’s packed complete by EX12 are single allocation detail oLPN’s . Multi-SKU oLPN packing performed by McLane executes through other means outside of EX12. The oLPN’s packed complete by EX12 are for full single UOM quantity. The user cannot partially pack an oLPN in this process. Alternate pick location capabilities are not used by McLane in full case picking scenarios and not in scope for this extension. Display of LPN Size/Type is not applicable to full case picking scenarios and not in scope for this extension. When custom printing at task level is enabled, WMOS forces the oLPN printing and packing to sequence by task detail id ( TASK_DTL.TASK_SEQ_NBR ) . No other sequencing is supported. Custom printing at task level is only supported in conjunction with EX12 custom packing . Other base available quantity confirmation options are not supported. If reprinting of oLPN labels is required, this is done through base available means in RF or UI. No additional custom printing is in scope to reprint any labels. If Shortages are chased against EX12 shorts, these are chased at the order level and not into the original oLPN . If printing at the task level, it is possible that some values on the oLPN shipping label may not be available as the oLPN’s are not yet packed. For EX12, RF Pack Cubed (Directed) assumes following configuration and ignores some base transaction parameters. Prompting for item attributes are not in scope. Disabling “End oLPN message” regardless of the configuration setup for the transaction parameter byte 39-39 . Base transaction parameter byte 26-26 – display location list is disabled . McLane does not track iLPNs in pick locations. McLane does not track serial numbers in its divisions. McLane does not perform catch-weight in its divisions. McLane does not leverage base WMOS zone picking. McLane full case flow does not leverage end oLPN feature since all the full case oLPNs are single case and they are auto-packed . McLane does not require picker id prompt for EX12 packing. Cycle count triggers (pick to zero and pick to threshold if configured) are configured automatically trigger without user input. Default reason code is setup to chase the shorted quantity and does not require user prompt. Default printer is setup and printer is not prompted w hen custom parameter byte 507-507 – task level printing is enabled. Dependencies Code Dependencies EX08 Customer Specific Lot Tracking – Possible that EX12 and EX08 must work in conjunction with one another. EX14 RF Picking – Display Task Header – Possible that EX12 and EX14 must work in conjunction with one another. EX18 Pack Cubed Directed Custom Pallet Prompt – Possible that EX12 and EX18 must work in conjunction with one another. Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Diagram Process Steps : Users initiate work in the appropriate task group by requesting work from WMOS through CTRL-S from the RF main menu. If the packing task assigned is not an EX12 packing task, then base process continues. Otherwise, WMOS considers which EX12 custom packing option is setup. Upon entering the EX 12 packing task, WMOS verifies if the transaction is configured for task level printing. If enabled, WMOS prints oLPN shipping labels for all oLPN’s associated to the task in pick location sequence and task detail sequence (TASK_DTL.TASK_SEQ_NBR) within pick location. Otherwise, if not configured for task level printing, WMOS continues to the item prompt. WMOS directs the user to the location and prompts the user to confirm the item. If user takes exception CTRL key for Location prompt instead, WMOS prompts the user for location and user scans location to confirm. Upon confirming location, WMOS packs all oLPNs from this task with the current item/pick location combination. WMOS forces the oLPN packing sequence to be in pick location sequence and oLPN detail sequence (TASK_DTL.TASK_SEQ_NBR) within pick location. Process ends, step 4/5 are omitted and step 6 occurs. Otherwise, user scans item to confirm. Upon confirming item, WMOS packs all oLPNs from this task with the current item/pick location combination. WMOS forces the oLPN packing sequence to be in pick location sequence and oLPN detail sequence (TASK_DTL.TASK_SEQ_NBR) within pick location. Process ends, step 4/5 are omitted and step 6 occurs. If user invokes Ctrl-Q to advance to total quantity prompt, user is b rought to step 4. WMOS prompts for item confirmation (displaying the same screen as step 3). If user takes exception CTRL key for Location prompt instead, WMOS prompts the user for location and user scans location to confirm. Here no custom packing updates occur, only validations that this is the correct location. Otherwise, user scans item to confirm. Here no custom packing updates occur, only validations that this is the correct item. At the total quantity prompt, WMOS displays the total demand for the task from that location and item and user enters the total quantity for packing. During packing, WMOS forces the oLPN packing sequence to be in pick location sequence and oLPN detail sequence (TASK_DTL.TASK_SEQ_NBR) within pick location. Upon successfully confirming item or quantity, WMOS validates if the transaction is configured for task level printing. If enabled, no further printing is required and WMOS contin ues to the next process. If task level printing was not enabled, then WMOS prints the oLPN Shipping Label for each oLPN packed complete. WMOS suppresses the oLPN packed complete messaging in this flow. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF RF Pack Cubed Directed Program Platform Available Text Based (RF Gun) Y UI N Mobile (DM) N Base RF Pack Cubed Directed transaction is enhanced with new quantity entry mode Item and Total Quantity. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portions of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX12 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX12 is turned off, and no custom EX12 processing will occur. Lastly, the custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled. If base transaction parameter 4-4 for Item / Quantity Verification Mode is set to ‘ 2’ –Item and Quantity and custom transaction parameter 502-502 is set to “Y” , then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. I f custom transaction parameter 502-502 is not “Y” or not setup a t all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues per base . Loading Task Task Validation A fter loading the task and after performing base validations , i f EX12 is enabled, then WMOS checks if all task details are single-unit (TASK_DTL. QTY_ALLOC =1). If not, WMOS displays custom error message CUST /120 1 “ Invalid Task: Task contains multi-unit detail. ” . Otherwise, WMOS proceeds with EX12 screen 1 flow. Navigation The navigation follows the base flow for error messages . If user accepts the message using Ctrl-A or backs out using Ctrl-W or exit s using Ctrl-X , user is taken to previous screen (example base error message : Invalid Task Group). Screen 1 – Item Prompt Prior to loading the Item Prompt screen when WMOS is configured for EX12 packing, first WMOS checks if the transaction is configured for task level printing. This is controlled through transaction parameter byte 50 7 - 50 7 which is configured as a check box Y/N flag. If the box is checked (Y) and base transaction parameter byte 3-3 enabled for printing , then WMOS automatically prints the oLPN shipping labels and or content labels for all oLPN’s on the task. WMOS prints the labels sequencing first by Pick Location Sequence and within pick location by task detail sequence ( TASK_DTL.TASK_SEQ_NBR ) ascending. If the box is unchecked (N) or not configured, WMOS continues with below processing without printing at the start of the task. This screen displays when the transaction Item and Quantity Verification Mode (Parameter 4-4) is set to ‘ 2’ – Item and Total Quantity. At each task detail, the item prompt is displayed first for the user to confirm. Note, this is a new custom screen built for EX12 purposes. Updates Upon successful Item Scan, WMOS performs base packing updates for the task’s oLPNs with this item/pick location combination . WMOS always packs the oLPNs in specific sequence. As the confirmations are by pick location, WMOS packs within the pick location by task detail sequence ( TASK_DTL.TASK_SEQ_NBR ) ascending. As part of that process, WMOS considers potentially printing the oLPN shipping labels. First, WMOS checks if the transaction is configured for task level printing. This is controlled through transaction parameter byte 507-507 which is configured as a check box Y/N flag. If the box is checked (Y), then WMOS printed the labels at t he start of the task and does not need to print the labels here. Otherwise, if the box is unchecked (N) or not configured, then WMOS calls printing for the oLPN Shipping Label for each oLPN packed complete. The multi- oLPN update includes weigh and manifest depending on the enablement of corresponding base parameters. If user executes the short (CTRL-D) exception, then WMOS performs base short updates for all pending allocations from this location and item combination on the user’s current task. The transaction configured default reason code is used to complete these updates. If user executes the Quantity Entry (Ctrl-Q) exception, no updates are performed. Navigation Upon successfully capturing the item, after packing completion WMOS continues to the next task detail. . If the user presses CTRL-D to short, after completing shortage updates, WMOS continues to the next task detail. If the user presses CTRL-L for Location Prompt, WMOS navigates to Screen 2 – Location Prompt. If the user presses CTRL-X to exit on this screen, WMOS returns the operator to the RF Menu. If the user presses Ctrl-Q to enter a quantity, WMOS prompts to confirm the item at S creen 3 – Item Confirmation . If the user presses Ctrl-K to skip, WMOS proceeds to the next task detail. EX 12 – Full Case Picking Changes McLane sells and ships full case quantities of items where each physical case quantity ships as an oLPN . Very often, these items pick from pallet or case flow pick locations which are Case Pick Locations in WMOS. Each unit picked is physically a case of inventory and systemically an oLPN . Today, McLane performs picking in these areas by labels with no real time system interaction. In future, McLane packs oLPN’s through RF Pack Cubed Directed. However, the base available confirmations either require too little detail (Location Only base option) or too many confirmations (SKU and Quantity for example). Therefore, WMOS RF Pack Cubed Directed is enhanced with a new item barcode prompt to pack the entire task detail and a new quantity confirmation option for McLane which allow the user to quickly confirm total or partial count packed. Additionally, the base process prints the oLPN S hipping L abel after the oLPN is packed complete by the user. McLane requires the flexibility to optionally print the oLPN Shipping Labels for all oLPN’s on a task at the start of the task. Therefore, WMOS RF Pack Cubed Directed is enhanced with a new configurable parameter to allow printing at the start of the task. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Pack Cubed Directed – New Quantity Confirmation by SKU and Aggregate Pick Need Reporting N Other N WMOS enhances RF Pack Cubed Directed to pack the aggregate demand of item/pick location combination of the current task at the item barcode prompt or proceed to total quantity prompt . If the user scans item barcode, the p rocess ends and the oLPNs are packed. If the user elects to move to the custom total quantity prompt, the process is to confirm the item barcode and view the total quantity prompt.

e current task at the item barcode prompt or proceed to total quantity prompt . If the user scans item barcode, the p rocess ends and the oLPNs are packed. If the user elects to move to the custom total quantity prompt, the process is to confirm the item barcode and view the total quantity prompt.

Th ese custom screen s present the user with two process es as part of this extension: Item Entry or Item and Total Quantity Entry . The initial confirmation of item leverages base item confirmation screen and base validations to confirm the user is picking the correct product , as well as performs custom updates to pack aggregate demand of task for current item/pick location combination . If the item barcode (or alternate options) or the custom Location scan is executed here, WM OS performs all packing updates for the oLPNs associated with this item and pick location combination for this task. WMOS also prints the shipping labels for the packed oLPNs per base. A new custom CTRL key is introduced at the item barcode scan, to move the user to the custom quantity confirmation screen. Once this CTRL key is invoked, the user will confirm the item, then n aggregate quantity across all picking demand from the given pick location and item on the user’s current task. WMOS performs all packing updates and shipping label printing for the quantity entered at this screen. This process allows the user to partially pack the aggregate demand from this item/pick location . As part of this picking confirmation, WMOS prints shipping labels for the packed oLPN’s per base. In order to support exceptions where item scan is not available, a new custom CTRL key is introduced at the item confirmation screen which allows the user to scan location instead. When taken, the CTRL key brings the user to a Location Confirmation screen which leverages base screen and validations to ensure the user is at the correct location for picking. Additionally, as part of this customization, WMOS suppress es oLPN confirmation in the RF Pack Cubed process. Lastly, in addition to the new quantity verification process, McLane requires the ability to optionally print oLPN Shipping Labels at the start of the task rather than as the oLPN packs complete. When configured for custom EX12 start of task printing, WMOS triggers printing for all oLPN’s on the task at the onset of the task. Additionally, WMOS forces the sequencing of both the printing and packing logic to ensure they are aligned. In addition, WMOS suppresses base oLPN ship label printing at the time of oLPN packing complete. Assumptions The base configuration for shortage reason code is configured to default without prompting the user. No customization to the oLPN shipping label which is printed in this process is in scope for this extension.

ppresses base oLPN ship label printing at the time of oLPN packing complete. Assumptions The base configuration for shortage reason code is configured to default without prompting the user. No customization to the oLPN shipping label which is printed in this process is in scope for this extension.

In some instances, McLane leverages the pallet prompt at the start of the packing process, but not always. This is configurable by task. Packing executes based on tasks created with the wave. Stand alone RF Pack Cubed Directed is not used by McLane outside tasking mode. The oLPN’s packed complete by EX12 are single allocation detail oLPN’s . Multi-SKU oLPN packing performed by McLane executes through other means outside of EX12. The oLPN’s packed complete by EX12 are for full single UOM quantity. The user cannot partially pack an oLPN in this process. Alternate pick location capabilities are not used by McLane in full case picking scenarios and not in scope for this extension. Display of LPN Size/Type is not applicable to full case picking scenarios and not in scope for this extension. When custom printing at task level is enabled, WMOS forces the oLPN printing and packing to sequence by task detail id ( TASK_DTL.TASK_SEQ_NBR ) . No other sequencing is supported. Custom printing at task level is only supported in conjunction with EX12 custom packing . Other base available quantity confirmation options are not supported. If reprinting of oLPN labels is required, this is done through base available means in RF or UI. No additional custom printing is in scope to reprint any labels. If Shortages are chased against EX12 shorts, these are chased at the order level and not into the original oLPN . If printing at the task level, it is possible that some values on the oLPN shipping label may not be available as the oLPN’s are not yet packed. For EX12, RF Pack Cubed (Directed) assumes following configuration and ignores some base transaction parameters. Prompting for item attributes are not in scope. Disabling “End oLPN message” regardless of the configuration setup for the transaction parameter byte 39-39 . Base transaction parameter byte 26-26 – display location list is disabled . McLane does not track iLPNs in pick locations. McLane does not track serial numbers in its divisions. McLane does not perform catch-weight in its divisions. McLane does not leverage base WMOS zone picking. McLane full case flow does not leverage end oLPN feature since all the full case oLPNs are single case and they are auto-packed . McLane does not require picker id prompt for EX12 packing. Cycle count triggers (pick to zero and pick to threshold if configured) are configured automatically trigger without user input.

Upon confirming location, WMOS packs all oLPNs from this task with the current item/pick location combination. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portions of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX12 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below.
